question_html:
Fill in the gaps using tag questions.

(i) Few students completed the assignment on time, ____

(ii) I think you’ve met my sister before, ____

(iii) Neither of the brothers was present at the event, ____

(iv) You’d rather stay home tonight, ____

(v) It’s high time we left for the airport, ____
solution_html:
(i) did they?

(ii) haven’t you?

(iii) were they?

(iv) wouldn’t you?

(v) isn’t it?

Mark Marks Distribution:

(0.5× 5) = 2.5 marks for writing the correct tag questions.

question_html:
Make sentences with the following group verbs:

carry on, look into, run out of, get over, cut down on
solution_html:
(i) Even after the interruption, the speaker decided to carry on with her presentation.

(ii) The police promised to look into the matter and provide an update soon.

(iii) We ran out of milk this morning, so I had to go to the store.

(iv) It took him months to get over the loss of his beloved pet.

(v) Doctors advise people to cut down on sugary drinks to improve their health.

Marks Distribution:
‎(0.5×5) = 2.5 marks for making sentences with group verbs.

question_html: Write a paragraph on ‘Domestic Violence’.
solution_html:
Domestic Violence

Domestic violence is a serious violation of human rights that occurs when one person in a relationship abuses another physically, emotionally, or psychologically. It affects individuals across all ages, genders, and social backgrounds, though women and children are often the most vulnerable. Victims of domestic violence suffer from physical injuries, mental trauma, and a loss of self-esteem, which can have long-term effects on their well-being and ability to lead normal lives. This harmful practice is often rooted in social inequality, cultural norms, and lack of awareness, while poverty, unemployment, and substance abuse can further increase its occurrence. Governments and organizations have introduced laws, awareness campaigns, and support services to protect victims and punish offenders. Shelters, counseling programs, and helplines play a vital role in helping survivors rebuild their lives. Ending domestic violence requires strong legal enforcement, community education, and a cultural shift towards respect and equality. Collective action from families, communities, and authorities can help create a safer and more just society.

Marks Distribution:

0.5 mark for writing, the Title "Domestic Violence".

02 marks for explaining, how domestic violence causes harm to victims and its bad impacts.

01 mark for writing, causes of the increase in domestic violence.

1.5 marks for writing, measures taken to end domestic violence.

[Instructions: *Students must write it within one single para. If it becomes more than one para, 50% marks have to be deducted from the deserved marks.

*0.5 mark will be deducted for one or two spelling mistakes, 01 mark for three or more spelling mistakes.]
